for_c_level: This run confirms low-risk posture with full pass on core functionality, supporting production readiness for mTLS-secured deployments; no regressions from prior runs, but S23/S24 highlight config and MCP issues targeted for Patch 2 closure. Customer claims around reliable multi-agent memory are viable at 100% scenario success. Maintain weekly cadence to track Patch 2 impact.
for_non_technical: Agents successfully shared and recalled memories across the network without any losses or mix-ups. Every test confirmed that information passed reliably between AI agents, even under stress like network splits or security checks. This run shows the system works as intended for keeping agent knowledge in sync.
for_sme: All 42 scenarios passed without failures, including S1-S42 covering transport (mTLS HTTP/gRPC), framework (federation sync, namespace isolation), and primitives (CRUD, hybrid recall, permissions, bulk ingest); S23 config tilde expansion and S24 MCP stdio bypass remain expected reds per Issues #507/#318, with no new failure modes observed—probable root causes unchanged, pending Patch 2 fixes.
headline: Ironclaw v0.6.3.1 achieves full scenario pass under mTLS federation
verdict: PASS — 42/42 scenarios green, known issues S23/S24 expected red
what_it_proved: Demonstrated reliable agent memory sharing, conflict resolution, and resilience to partitions/deletes with 100% recall accuracy and zero unauthorized access in all tested axes.
what_it_tested: Exercised 42 scenarios covering memory CRUD, federation replication, hybrid recall, permissions, pubsub, bulk ops, and security primitives across mTLS transport in 4-node mesh topology.
